payload.purpose_context: End users install Spec Kitty into real projects and operate it through AI harnesses such as Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Gemini, and Pi TUI. Today the public docs mix 1.x, 2.x, 3.1, and 3.2 material, the CLI reference lags the live Typer tree (only 113 of 192 visible paths are covered), and there is no consistent install/upgrade/uninstall guidance across pip, pipx, and uv on macOS, Linux, and Windows. This mission produces a Divio-structured 3.2 documentation set, a generator-backed CLI reference, harness research with explicit support classification, and version-leakage validation so that adopters and existing users land on accurate, version-correct guidance for the upcoming 3.2 releases.
payload.purpose_tldr: Make Spec Kitty 3.x/3.2 docs the complete current source of truth, archive 1.x/2.x, rebuild the CLI reference from the live command tree, and cover every supported AI harness end-to-end.

options: 3.1 surfaced as supported version with its own nav group
3.1 folded into 3.2 as migration notes only
payload.question: Should the public 3.x docs expose 3.1 as a separately navigable supported version, or treat 3.1 pages purely as migration notes folded under 3.2?
Should the CLI reference be hand-maintained markdown, fully generated from the live Typer tree by a committed script, or hybrid (generated body with hand-authored wrapper prose)?

options: All 15 currently-installed harness directories ship as first-class
Tiered: first-class for Claude/Codex/Cursor/OpenCode/Gemini, supported for the rest
Author the matrix first, then promote per-harness pages based on coverage
payload.question: Which AI harnesses ship as officially supported for 3.2 (first-class/supported) vs opportunistically documented (partial/experimental/archived) — and is the per-harness page set the canonical list or only the support matrix?

payload.rationale: Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until plan phase so the page-classification taxonomy and navigation design address it together.

Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until methodology investigation of prior reference commits (a14769e7a, 81b3d6c3e, 514106af2, deee8d7f3) and CLI audit decide whether a generator is justified.
Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until harness research workstream surveys current host integration depth and Spec Kitty's generated files for each.
